# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

Deno.serve(async (req) => {
  try {
    // Request network permissions at runtime for maximum flexibility
    await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" });

    const url = new URL(req.url);
    const targetUrl = url.searchParams.get("url");

    if (!targetUrl) {
      return new Response("Missing 'url' query parameter.", { status: 400 });
    }

    // Validate the target URL to ensure it's a well-formed URL
    let parsedTargetUrl: URL;
    try {
      parsedTargetUrl = new URL(targetUrl);
    } catch (e) {
      return new Response("Invalid URL provided.", { status: 400 });
    }

    // Perform the fetch request
    const response = await fetch(parsedTargetUrl.toString());

    // Check if the fetch was successful
    if (!response.ok) {
      return new Response(`Failed to fetch URL: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`, { status: response.status });
    }

    // Return the fetched content with the original content type
    const contentType = response.headers.get("Content-Type") || "application/octet-stream";
    return new Response(response.body, {
      headers: { "Content-Type": contentType },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error during fetch operation:", error);
    // Handle specific permission error
    if (error instanceof Deno.errors.PermissionDenied) {
      return new Response("Network permission was denied. Cannot fetch content.", { status: 403 });
    }
    return new Response(`Internal server error: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
});
